string.text: {=mFiGSOSm}Certainly. The more who know about my skills, the better.

{=8lQ9IM8N}The women in our family passed down the art of healing from generation to generation, and other... em... services, that can be accomplished with herbs. But for all our skill we remained poor, while richly robed, well-fed men with half our skill walked about the towns tending to the lords and ladies.

{=dKAun6OE}By the time I became a woman I was sick of filthy huts and puking babies. I came to the towns and inquired after which households, among those who had wealth, might have a patient for me. Of course when I showed up at the door in my torn, stained dress they slammed it in my face.

{=WvLK5myZ}So I acquired some silver - I will not say how - and with it bought a silk robe and some jewelry. This got me inside the doors of rich men's houses, and the cures I worked, and the reputation I earned, have done the rest.
{=Zb6ObDEB}Sounds like you have a good head for business.
{=C19gsGFY}Yes - many is the healer who has come to specialize in the diseases of the rich.

{=jcrmV9Lg}But I've noticed something - more coin is spent on war than on anything else. So, if someone's raised an army, and doesn't want to lose their investment to infected wounds and fevers, well, it might be worthwhile to employ me.
{=qyvj53mi}Once I was just another back-country healer, attending to squalling babies and superstitious villagers. But now I'm trying to make the money that my skill with medicine deserves.
{=lGSH1wsv}Pull up a chair, friend - you'll be glad you asked!

{=7TbLW9lr}I could tell you of all my deeds, and you'd never believe me. But you know, if you'd met me a year back, I could have taken you to my hall some time and shown you them. Shown you my deeds! You heard that!

{=yj3nO24m}I do things in the old way, and so whenever an enemy falls to my spear, I take their head! Let's see - I have Arnold the Old, Reginald the Lame -- don't let the names fool you, these were great warriors - Aymer the Bull, so many others! A true Scotsman never boasts, because his deeds are hung over the hearth for all to see. At least that's the way it used to be.

{=mPLvjSiB}A lot of heads... You can imagine, a lot of other warriors were envious of me. Some in my village even conspired against me, had me expelled. I lost my hall, and I had to bury the heads. I doubt you could recognize them any more. What a pity, eh? A collection like that will never be seen again.
{=ZqWHeJ9B}Envy, yes, the only possible explanation for why your clan drove you out. Such a terrible sin!
{=tRgVxROs}I doubt you have such a collection, and if you did, it would be the last thing I'd like to see.
{=OESUaPeh}So anyway, I need money to build a new hall, and start a new collection. So if someone wants to pay me my worth as a warrior, my spear is theirs!

{=LDlFgmIs}Well now, I am a warrior and a true Scot. There is no greater boast than that, I will tell you! But perhaps if you hire me, I can tell you more later about the great names I have slain and the heads I have taken.
{=APcr8rgq}I'll tell you. You may wish to get yourself a drink.

{=t0VGwvPo}You may have heard of my father - Ednyfed Fychan. Many called him Ednyfed the Brave. It's my life's goal to be his worthy son, though he died when I was but a boy. My stepfather, though rich in land and cows, does not have the same reputation, although I resolved early in my life that duty to my mother necessitated that I serve him as loyally as I would my natural father.

{=a1FYCbdy}Soon after I came of age, we got wind that our neighbors would raid us for cattle. We did not know by which road they would come. I was sent with a few of our tenants to hold a ford that was far out of the way.

{=iGH88WOz}At first I was angered, because I believed that I was being kept away from battle and glory. But as it turned out, that's the way the enemy army came. When I saw them at the ford, I challenged their leaders to single combat, as that was our way. They accepted, and sent forth a champion. To make a long story short, I slew him, and then two more who came after him. The rest graciously admitted that my victories meant they were obliged to retreat.
{=QRgltY66}May all of us have such honorable foes!
{=egZ6P7sq}A boy like you killed three champions, eh? Well, all things are possible, I suppose.

{=d8aCwUJj}When I returned to my stepfather's hall, my mother met me outside, in tears. She had given me up for dead - my stepfather knew the direction from which the enemy clan would come, and had intended for me to die. She did nothing to stop him, but since I had survived, she bid me flee - for if not, she feared, it was inevitable that our clan should sink into the abyss of kinslaying. So here I am, looking for a worthy lord for whom to fight.

{=7bX48G5w}I was raised in a well-known clan, but my father died and my stepfather cast me out. It is a common enough story among our people. You may find many of us wandering the roads, our swords our only inheritance.
{=4qqsndje}One moment. I need a drink.

{=wH5GepOD}I am English. But for 20 years, I fought for many other kings and lords in Europe.. Indeed, many English boys learned their trade in the retinues of European lords. We saw no dishonor in it. They fed us well, they paid us well, they respected our courage.

{=WmGxA0P1}But there's no more of that now. Since Henry became king, well, he's gotten the young to think that to fight for any but the English is treason. Treason to what? 'poland'? There is no poland. Never has been. Just a bunch of quarreling warlords, who'll steal your cows as soon as you turn your back on them.

{=7QFCINsI}Every time I've fought with a band of my 'countrymen,' I've told them: 'Hold your lines. Stay firm. Fight as the Romans did: as a solid hammer, not a gaggle of goats.' And every time I have seen them disintegrate into a screaming rush as soon as the arrows start flying, to dash themselves on the enemy's shields or be cut down by cavalry coming in on the sides.
{=VnfPb4ji}Indeed. There's much to be learned from the foreigner, whoever the foreigner is to you.
{=F9dSDE2k}A man should fight for his hearth and his kin, however much they disappoint him.
{=HREVUExb}Anyway, well, I've had it with narrow escapes from charging French knights, and I'm going to seek work in a more professional force.
{=688YMH2y}I am English by birth but I served all over Europe. Many of us did. There is as much honor serving a German count, or French baron as any other master.
{=OxBmSnh0}I shall tell you. Is there a bard in the house? He may wish to take notes. I believe this is fine material for a song.
{=9vt4wew9}I am a connoisseur of horseflesh, my friend. No one knows horses better than I. But I find, alas, that often the best horses are wasted on unworthy owners, so I try to rectify that.

{=VUbklG5U}I was raised as a stableboy. But I had a gift for breaking horses, and for my hard work, and the risks I took to my neck taming young stallions, the old lord promised me that on my fifteenth birthday I should have a horse of my own.

{=zwKDTWd5}But the old lord died, alas, and when my birthday came, his heir laughed at me. He told me that a fine horse would be wasted on me; perhaps I would like a goat. So that night I took the best animal in the stable, and rode off.
{=bagbVij6}So, what you're saying is that you're a horsethief.
{=ysfnd0id}Of course. Every felon is just an honest man wronged. I've heard a hundred such tales and I've no wish to hear another.

{=KHq1bUOa}I am the prince of horsethieves! I have stolen Arabic mares from amid the tents and ridden them across the desert under the moon, I have taken French stallions from their paddocks, the sturdy ponies of the Polish, and the steady geldings of the German lancers. But, I'll be the first to admit, it is risky work, so sometimes I do other jobs.

{=ZRQIdapZ}I am a horsethief. I come from a region where to be a horsethief is an honorable profession, and I am very good at it. But it is dangerous work, as owners can be vindictive people, and I am seeking safer work - as a mercenary, perhaps.
{=LzlRFPrj}Give me a minute. It has been a long time since I spoke more than a few words at a time to others.

{=84coQmIJ}My family was not one of the greatest houses in poland, but neither are we among the poorest. When I came of age, my family sent me to join a lord's army on campaign. You may have heard of this tradition - for three years we live in tents and eat porridge, march in uniform, and barely see any fighting!
{=re1jRErx}Some speak well of their time brought up in a retinue. It is intended to teach you the ways of soldiering. But the 'unit' I joined were all rich children, playing at being soldier.

{=U6GQq4Id}They would sit around the fire at night and belittle each other. I was never quick with my tongue and the others learned to gang up on me. At one point I could take it no longer - I stabbed one in a fight. No one knew how to treat the wound, so he bled to death. And then I fled.
{=HtbAEEcR}You had little choice it seems.
{=fynTmZiX}So you are a deserter? Just when you think you've heard it all... Good day to you.

{=jp9iAxab}I saw how much tradition was worth. I knew I had to answer for my crimes, and I heard soon enough that the family of the youth I'd stabbed hired men to kill me. I stayed in the woods, living alone. I am happy there, most of the time. But sometimes I yearn to be among other men, and come to the city to look for work.
{=bZ1qYeIa}I'm a former soldier but I was forced to desert to save my own life. I am not proud of what I did. But I am proud of the craft I learned.
{=rriedqBz}Oh, I'll tell you. It's a fine tale, full of adventure and romance, but tragic too.

{=IRTr1FXk}Aye, once I made a fine living, in the capital, stopping merchants on the roads and asking 'em for a bit of change. But there was a lass there, beautiful as the moon and just as cold, and she was my undoing. Courted by an noble lad but I thought, I can do better than that, with my takings, and I brought her the pick of the rings and finery and asked her to pass the evening with me.

{=oAyYQazn}She agreed, eagerly enough! There was wine and talk, but too much wine, and I slept off. Then woke in the morning, I did, and hooves were clattering outside and up comes the lad and a file of horsemen, and away they took me to the town to be hanged.

{=oqWFzmkN}Blessed be the Lord, happened that my cousin Philip was in the guard. He sprung me that night from the prison, and together we went roaming round the country. But a passing sheriff decided he weren't parting with his purse, and pulled his blade rather than handing it over like a sensible lad. I took him down, but not before my poor Philip was butchered. See now the price of woman's ingratitude?
{=hGL3LHSN}Most unlucky you were.
{=GCd02obf}Perhaps one should not press a courtship too far, when one is a bandit.
{=tqdwOLPg}So here I am now, drinking to my poor cousin's memory, and looking for a bit of honest work. Well, it needn't be too honest, I suppose.

{=TD3babBS}I was a robber on the highways, in London, making a fine living from the merchants on the roads. But I loved a girl in town, and snuck in to visit her from time to time. Then I got caught, and had to reveal the hiding place all my hard-earned loot to the guards to escape the noose. So here I am now.
{=yHWwzEZQ}Aye, I can tell you my story.

{=0reAi03W}I was a cowherd. In most villages, this is a boy's job, because one must be alone in the hills. In our village there were few boys, and I was a good shot with a sling, good at keeping the wolves away.

{=12lOrxfb}The boys of the neighboring village would pester me. One of them, the elder's son, would not leave me alone. They wanted from me what all boys want. One day he came up behind me and tried to take me by force. But I had a knife...
{=EtfElSU3}I did not know what to do with his body. I buried it up in the hills, then told my parents. For fear of bloodshed, they told me to leave, to find my way in the world some other way.
{=bYrgTQ3s}It sounds as though you had no choice.
{=OUTjQlW1}There are customs for that sort of thing, trials, blood money, you know.
{=mxK0bSmE}I am no warrior, not yet - but I could be one. I am good with a sling, and a knife. I can run fast. I can bear hardship. I would stand my ground, because I have nowhere to run.

{=pabl5PiA}I was a shepherd. Normally the job is given to boys, but I always had a knack for bows and slings, which we use to keep the wolves away. The trouble with being a shepherd, though, is that there's always the chance of quarrels with the herders of the neighboring village over disputed pastures or missing sheep. Perhaps I was too good with a bow - I killed a neighbor. My parents were poor and could not afford the blood money, so I fled.
{=ZOVdtwbw}So, you want to know about the games that Fate has played with me?

{=qFU6cYSe}When I was just a baby, a woman said to have the Sight came to my father's hall. When she saw me, she told him that she saw the mark of the faeries upon me. She performed a divination, and said she had learned that I was to be a prank played on mankind: I should grow up to be a great beauty, but that any man who proposes to marry me shall die.

{=aZOuFJ6H}Well, he did not believe in such things, so he laughed, tossed her a few coins, and sent her on her way. I grew up. When my first suitor was skewered by a spear in battle, I grieved, but thought little of it.
{=P1EHkSAx}When my second suitor passed of a fever, I was unnerved. When my third suitor was thrown from his horse on a hunt, I knew I must do something..
{=KbdCL6nK}Very responsible of you. Can't go killing off the whole village, now.
{=7RyQGcbI}The trouble with prophecies... People remember the one that came true, never the ten that were false.

{=gPN2xlx1}I resolved to take up the profession of arms, so that all men know that I am not the marrying type. I forge my own weapons, to avoid going into town whenever possible. And I tell all others of the curse upon me, as soon as I meet them. And I am far from my family, so no man will go to my father to ask for my hand.

{=bhVekK9J}I come from the highlands. My people were suspicious folk. It is easy to them to get into their heads that a child, especially a girl, practices witchcraft. I had two neighbors die of fevers within the same season, and so I was chased from my home and driven to fend for myself. Which I can, and did. I forge my own weapons and can wield them as well, but now I'm looking for steady work.
{=l01koCnM}My story? This is my tale in a nutshell - men's greed for cattle cost me my home and my kin.

{=DZTOovh6}My uncle - he had a great herd of cows, and in the English upcountry that is only kind of wealth we value. But they were taken by his chief herdsman, a man we trusted. So we took up spears to get them back, but my uncle was now poor, but the thief had many kinsmen. Having nothing else to lose, my uncle ordered that even the women of our family take up spears and ride out to fight, and so we did.
{=RBwhiMkD}We came down on the thief's village one day at dawn. They were too many, and we were beaten. My brothers and most of my kin were all slain. I had nowhere to go.

{=ECaJe7d8}But I had killed three men with a spear that day, and found I had a taste for it. And I learned that thieves grow rich, while honest men die. So I lived in the hills, and took the cows of families more fortunate then my own. But I sold the cows I took, and kept the denars close to my chest.
{=4u5YQtKn}Hard to blame you for that, I suppose.
{=8U01rj5f}When a victim turns thief, that does not right the world.
{=fkbaYOGt}But I don't intend to live this way forever. There are a thousand paths to turning outlaw, you see, but they mostly come to a single end. So I'm here, in the town, looking for other work.

{=0ttlPDSr}My family was in a feud. Over stolen cattle, as is often the case in the upcountry. Well, the family we fought turned out to be rather stronger than us, and most of us were killed. The rest were scattered. I lived for a while as a thief, stealing to eat, but that is a bitter path, and now I am looking for something better.
